macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge 3% to $90 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates, Raising Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Brent crude surpassed $90 a barrel on Monday after the U.S. and Iran expanded attacks in the Middle East, curbing energy shipments in the Strait of Hormuz and driving oil prices up 3% amid fears of renewed inflation.

items.title: Canada Inflation Cools to 2.8% in June, Below Expectations as Gasoline Prices Fall
items.summary: Canada's annual inflation rate dropped to 2.8% in June, cooler than forecast, driven by sharply lower gasoline costs following the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding to end the war.

items.title: NY Fed Survey: Credit Application Rate Hits Five-Year High in June
items.summary: Americans' application rate for new credit reached its highest level in nearly five years in June, according to a new Federal Reserve Bank of New York survey released Monday.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company with $4.88 Trillion Valuation
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot as the world's most valuable company, reaching a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%.

items.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise Slightly; Chipmakers in Focus Ahead of Key Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures rose slightly on Monday following last week's chip-stocks-led pullback, as investors await earnings that could test Wall Street's AI-driven rally amid Middle East tensions.

items.title: European Shares Edge Down on Oil Price Spike and Inflation Worries
items.summary: European shares declined slightly on Monday as escalating U.S.-Iran conflict drove oil prices higher, stoking inflation concerns ahead of the corporate earnings season.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh: Fed Will Set Interest Rates Without Political Consideration
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh stated the Fed will continue to set interest rates independently of politics during his first congressional testimony on Tuesday.

items.title: S&P 500 Futures Up 0.37%; DAX Flat as Markets Await Earnings and Oil Volatility
items.summary: The S&P 500 index futures gained 0.37% while the DAX remained flat (+0.06%) as markets brace for earnings season and volatile oil prices driven by Middle East conflict.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $64,000 Amid AI Selloff and Oil Volatility, Though Weekly Gains Persist
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 1.3% on Monday to approximately $63,900 after failing to hold above $65,000, pressured by an AI-driven chip stock selloff and rising oil prices from Middle East tensions, though it remains up 2% for the week[1][14]. Ethereum fell 3.98% to $1,828, while XRP, Dogecoin, and BNB also moved sideways or declined slightly[1][2][8].

items.title: Bitcoin and Ether ETFs Surge with $239M Inflows as Japan Advances Crypto ETF Framework
items.summary: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted over $181 million and Ether ETFs saw $58.34 million in inflows this week, marking a strong recovery after June outflows[8]. Japan advanced its regulated crypto ETF framework, fueling optimism for institutional adoption[8].

items.title: U.S. and Senate Push to Pass CLARITY Act Before August Recess; Trump to Meet Senators on Crypto Bill
items.summary: President Donald Trump is scheduled to meet senators on Thursday to discuss the CLARITY Act, with lawmakers aiming to pass the market structure bill before the August congressional recess[7]. The bill aims to clarify regulatory oversight for digital assets in the U.S.[7].

items.title: Russia’s Parliament Holds Final Readings on Crypto Bill Tuesday; Vietnam Sets Fines for Unlicensed Trading
items.summary: Russia’s State Duma will hold final readings on its crypto regulation bill Tuesday, including rules for investors and cross-border payments[6][9]. Vietnam established fines up to $1,900 for unlicensed crypto trading and AML breaches ahead of its regulated market launch[6][9].

items.title: Pi Network Upgrades to V25 Protocol on July 22 with Zero-Knowledge and Privacy Features
items.summary: Pi Network will upgrade to the V25 protocol on July 22, introducing BN254 cryptography, Poseidon hashing, zero-knowledge proofs, and privacy-focused smart contracts[7]. The update will first deploy on testnet before mainnet rollout[7].

items.title: Bitmine Expands Ethereum Treasury to 5.78M ETH; Grayscale Plans Cash Payouts from ETH and SOL Staking
items.summary: Bitmine repurchased 5.5 million shares and expanded its Ethereum treasury to 5.78 million ETH[4]. Grayscale announced regular cash distributions from staking rewards generated by its Ether and Solana exchange-traded products[4][6].

items.title: Hyperliquid’s HIP-4 Requires 500,000 HYPE Stake for Permissionless Prediction Markets
items.summary: Hyperliquid will require developers to stake 500,000 HYPE (worth ~$30.4 million) to deploy permissionless prediction markets under HIP-4[9]. The protocol also supports outcome markets with permissionless deployment[4].

items.title: France Blocks Polymarket Access; SEC Targets Mining Automatic Over $22M Crypto Mining Fraud
items.summary: France blocked access to prediction market platform Polymarket as part of a crackdown on crypto prediction markets[10]. The SEC charged Mining Automatic with alleged $22 million crypto mining fraud[5].

items.title: Cardano Activates van Rossem Hard Fork; Solana Price Stalls Below $80 Amid Exploits
items.summary: Cardano successfully activated the van Rossem hard fork, enhancing network capabilities[6]. Solana’s price stalled below $80 as recent exploits tested its fragile recovery[5].

items.title: IREN Shares Climb Double Digits After Securing $2.8B AI Cloud Contracts; Hut 8 Commercializes 1GW Texas AI Campus
items.summary: IREN shares surged after securing $2.8 billion in AI cloud contracts and raising its 2026 revenue target[4][8]. Hut 8 fully commercialized its 1 GW Texas AI campus with a second $9.8 billion lease[4].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Pauses Kimi K3 Subscriptions After Demand Overwhelms Capacity
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI temporarily halted new subscriptions for its newly launched Kimi K3 model, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight system claiming frontier-adjacent performance, due to overwhelming demand straining capacity just days after release.

items.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8 Max, Claiming It's Second Only to Anthropic's Fable 5
items.summary: Alibaba Group previewed its new Qwen3.8 Max AI model with 2.4 trillion parameters, asserting it ranks as the world's second-most powerful model, trailing only Anthropic's frontier Fable 5, signaling China's rapid progress in AI.

items.title: Google Developing Server Chip Embedding Gemini Model Elements for Efficiency
items.summary: Google is developing a new server chip that incorporates elements of its Gemini AI model directly into hardware to serve AI models more efficiently to users, according to The Information.

items.title: Tempus AI Acquires Personalis in $1.5B All-Stock Deal for AI Precision Medicine
items.summary: Tempus AI announced an all-stock acquisition of Personalis valued at approximately $1.5 billion, adding a cancer test to its AI-enabled precision medicine oncology portfolio.

items.title: Hut 8 Secures $9.8B Lease to Fully Commercialize 1GW Texas AI Data Center
items.summary: Hut 8, a crypto-mining company pivoted to AI data centers, signed a second 15-year lease worth $9.8 billion with an investment-grade customer, fully commercializing its 1-gigawatt Beacon Point campus in Texas.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Embed Series 8B, Tops RTEB Retrieval Benchmark
items.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 Embed Series 8B version, which achieved top performance on the RTEB retrieval benchmark, advancing its embedded AI capabilities.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Launches First In-House Model 'Inkling'
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, an AI startup co-founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, launched its first in-house AI model named Inkling.

items.title: AMD Launches Helios Rack AI System to Rival Nvidia, Adds Microsoft as Buyer
items.summary: AMD launched Helios, its first rack-scale AI system designed to compete with Nvidia's offerings, and announced Microsoft as its newest buyer.

items.title: TSMC Announces $100B Investment to Expand Arizona AI Chip Facilities
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), the world's largest advanced AI chip maker, announced a massive $100 billion investment to expand its facilities in Arizona.

items.title: Meta Pulls Instagram AI Feature Amid Criticism of Account Misuse
items.summary: Meta disabled a recently launched AI tool feature after criticism that it enabled Instagram accounts to be used as fodder for creating AI-generated images.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

news.title: Palworld 1.0 Surges to Over 950K Concurrent Players, Briefly Overtaking Counter-Strike 2 on Steam
news.summary: Following its July 10 version 1.0 launch, Palworld climbed back into Steam's all-time top 15 with 921,485 concurrent players, briefly surpassing Counter-Strike 2 in peak concurrency.

news.title: UK Game Store GAME Falls Into Administration with £16 Million Debt
news.summary: Major UK video game retailer GAME has entered administration due to £16 million in debt, marking a significant blow to the physical gaming retail sector.

news.title: MXGP 26 Announced for October 2026 Release on PC and Consoles
news.summary: Nacon and Artefacts Studio revealed MXGP 26: The Official Game, built on Unreal Engine 5, with a release window set for October 2026 on PS5, Xbox Series, and PC.

news.title: Call of Duty Movie Confirmed to Be Set in Infinity Ward's Modern Warfare Timeline
news.summary: The upcoming Call of Duty film will take place within the Modern Warfare timeline established by Infinity Ward, aligning the movie with the franchise's core narrative.

news.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Surpasses Three Million Copies Sold
news.summary: The remastered version of Assassin's Creed: Black Flag has sold over three million copies worldwide, demonstrating strong demand for the classic franchise.

news.title: Marathon Loses Game Director Joe Ziegler Amid Bungie Staffing Changes
news.summary: Bungie's Marathon has lost Game Director Joe Ziegler and General Manager, with new internal promotions announced to fill the leadership gaps.

news.title: GTA VI Hacker Faces Retrial After Hospital Order Lifted
news.summary: The individual responsible for hacking GTA VI is set for a retrial after an indefinite hospital order was lifted, renewing legal proceedings against the hacker.

news.title: PlayStation Offers Free Year of PS Plus Premium to Some Current Subscribers
news.summary: Sony has begun distributing a free year of PS Plus Premium to select current subscribers as part of a new promotional initiative.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SpaceX Starship Launch Aborted Last-Moment on Pad
items.summary: SpaceX's next Starship test flight was aborted at the last second on the launch pad, delaying the mission until at least next week.

items.title: Commodore Launches Callback 8020 Retro Flip Phone
items.summary: The reborn Commodore brand entered the phone market with the Callback 8020, a retro flip phone running Android apps via privacy-focused Sailfish OS while blocking social media and browsers.

items.title: Sony Unveils Reon Pocket Pro Plus Wearable Air Conditioner
items.summary: Sony improved its wearable cooling device with the Reon Pocket Pro Plus, offering a better fit and stronger cooling that can raise or lower skin temperature by several degrees.

items.title: Amazon Leo Satellite Internet Service to Launch in South Africa in 2027
items.summary: Amazon announced it will launch its new satellite internet service, Amazon Leo, in South Africa in 2027, potentially beating Elon Musk's Starlink in the region.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Additional $100 Billion for US Chip Manufacturing Expansion
items.summary: Taiwan's major chipmaker TSMC plans to invest another $100 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: Alibaba's AliExpress Hit with Record €550 Million EU Fine
items.summary: The European Union fined Alibaba's AliExpress €550 million ($629 million) for failing to prevent sales of illegal, unsafe, and counterfeit products on its platform.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide One-Year Moratorium on Large Data Centers
items.summary: New York Governor blocked new large data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium.

items.title: Meta Faces Trial in Tennessee Over Instagram's Design and Youth Mental Health
items.summary: Meta Platforms began a trial in Tennessee where the state claims Instagram's design is responsible for a youth mental-health crisis, part of broader allegations of intentionally addictive platform design.

items.title: World's First Fully Robot-Staffed Hotel to Open in China in 2027
items.summary: China will open the world's first hotel entirely staffed by robots in 2027 on the artificial island of the Shenzhen-Zhongshan Link megaproject.

items.title: Valeo to Produce Electric Drone Motors for Defense Firm Harmattan in France
items.summary: Automotive supplier Valeo announced it will develop and produce electric drone motors in France for defense technology firm Harmattan AI.

items.title: India's CDSL Fined 10 Million Rupees for Cybersecurity Failures
items.summary: India's markets regulator fined Central Depository Services (India) Ltd 10 million rupees for cybersecurity and compliance failures linked to a 2022 malware attack.

items.title: Apple Reclaims Title as World's Most Valuable Company, Passing Nvidia
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia to become the world's most valuable company again, reclaiming the top spot for the first time since April 2025.
